big_picture:
• What are the Chola plates? Netherlands returns 1,000-year-old Indian heritage to PM Modi — 6 sources
• At UN, India flags concerns over targeting of vessels in Strait of Hormuz — 6 sources
• Tata Electronics and ASML partner on India’s first semiconductor fab — 5 sources
• Chandrababu Naidu Announces Up To Rs 40,000 Cash Incentives In Population Push: Report — 5 sources
• Indus Waters Treaty: India rejects arbitration award, calls it ‘null and void’ — 5 sources
• Can PM Modi's visit to UAE, other four countries find a solution to the soaring oil prices problem? — 5 sources
• President Murmu promulgates ordinance to increase Supreme Court judge strength by four — 4 sources
• CNG Price Hiked by Rs 1 Per kg in Delhi-NCR, Second Rise in Two Days — 4 sources
• Fire breaks out in coach of Rajdhani Express, no casualties — 4 sources
• Case filed against TMC’s Abhishek Banerjee for  ‘provocative’ speeches during poll campaign — 4 sources

story_clusters.summary: The Netherlands returned the 11th-century Century Chola Copper Plates to India during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit, marking the culmination of a 14-year effort. The repatriation was announced by multiple sources, including IE India, ThePrint Diplomacy, and Deccan Chronicle, highlighting its significance as a historic homecoming. The event coincided with Modi’s meeting with the Dutch King, as reported by CNBC Latest.

story_clusters.summary: India, Israel, and Lebanon have extended a ceasefire for 45 days following talks in Washington, while the UAE is advancing plans to complete a second oil pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz by 2027. The UAE aims to double its oil exports by 2027 through this pipeline, reducing reliance on the Strait of Hormuz. Additionally, India has raised concerns at the UN about the targeting of vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Supreme Court Quarterly Digest 2026 - Labour Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the BOCW Act and Cess Act, enacted in 1996, remained largely unimplemented in many states until Welfare Boards were constituted under Section 18. The Court held that the lack of machinery (Welfare Boards) led to the dormancy of these laws. The ruling highlights the need for state-level enforcement of labour welfare provisions for construction workers.
articles.raw_summary: Labour Law - Supreme Court Quarterly Digest Jan - Mar, 2026 Building and Other Construction Workers (Regulation of Employment and Conditions of Service) Act, 1996 (BOCW Act) and The Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Cess Act, 1996 (Cess Act) — Implementation and Levy: Held, that although the BOCW Act and Cess Act were enacted in 1996, they remained "dormant" in various states until the necessary machinery, specifically the Welfare Boards under Section 18, was constituted - The con

articles.title: Kerala HC Upholds Rejection of Delayed Income Tax Refund Claim Due to Five-Year CBDT Limit

articles.title: When Finality Fails: Res Judicata After Setting Aside Of Awards
articles.summary: The article examines the application of the legal principle of res judicata in arbitration cases, particularly after an arbitral award is set aside under Section 34 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The Supreme Court has ruled that issues once decided cannot be re-agitated across arbitral stages, but the implications of setting aside an award remain unclear. This uncertainty could impact future arbitration proceedings and legal interpretations in India.
articles.raw_summary: Though the incorporation of the principle of res judicata in arbitration is well settled, its application becomes uncertain once an arbitral award is set aside under Section 34 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (hereinafter referred as 'the Arbitration Act'). articles.title: Delhi High Court Issues Notice On PIL Challenging Consumer Protection Act Provisions Relating To  'Substantial Question Of Law'
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court , comprising Chief Justice Devendra Kumar Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia , has issued notice to the Union of India in a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) challenging the constitutional validity of provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019. The plea challenges provisions which vest the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) with the power to adjudicate “substantial questions of law” through benches that may include technical and non-judicial members.

articles.title: State Cannot Deny Benefits Under Final Order Merely Because Employees Delayed Seeking Its Implementation : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Andhra Pradesh government cannot deny benefits to Grade-IV employees under a final court order simply because the employees delayed seeking its implementation. The bench emphasized that the government, as a model employer, cannot exploit its own failure to comply with the law. The ruling came in an appeal filed by a group of employees against the state and Visakhapatnam authorities.

articles.title: Supreme Court Quarterly Digest 2026 - Contract Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India, in its Quarterly Digest for January-March 2026, ruled on the interpretation of contracts, emphasizing the 'literal rule' over 'purposive construction.' The Court stated that if contract terms are clear, the parties' intention must be derived directly from the text, and reliance on post-contract conduct is improper. This ruling clarifies how courts should interpret contracts in future disputes.

articles.title: Clever Drafting Cannot Save a Benami Claim: Supreme Court on Meaningful Reading of Plaint Under Order 7 Rule 11
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that clever drafting cannot save a benami claim, directing the confiscation of a property where a claim based on a will was actually an attempt to obtain judicial recognition of a benami transaction. The Court emphasized the need for a meaningful reading of the plaint under Order 7 Rule 11 of the Civil Procedure Code. This decision underscores the judiciary's stance on preventing misuse of legal processes to conceal benami transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court directed the confiscation of the property where a claim, which was presented to be based on a will, but was in substance an attempt to obtain judicial recognition of a benami transaction. The post Clever Drafting Cannot Save a Benami Claim: Supreme Court on Meaningful Reading of Plaint Under Order 7 Rule 11 appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Interplay of GST & Criminal Law: A Judicial Scrutiny of FIRs & Prosecutions
articles.summary: Courts have ruled that GST offences under the CGST Act must adhere to the special procedures mandated by the law. Authorities cannot bypass these safeguards, such as prior sanction for prosecution, by invoking general IPC provisions. This judgment clarifies the interplay between GST regulations and criminal law, ensuring stricter compliance with tax-related prosecutions.

articles.title: CPC Cannot Change AOP Status to Co-operative Society in ITR Processing: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that the Centralized Processing Centre (CPC) cannot unilaterally reclassify an apartment owners' association (AOP) as a co-operative society while processing income tax returns under Section 143(1). The tribunal overturned a tax demand of Rs. 97,690, stating that such adjustments exceed the scope of prima facie processing. The decision clarifies the limits of CPC's authority in tax return processing and may influence future tax policies
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that CPC cannot suo motu reclassify an apartment owners association as a co-operative society while processing returns under Section 143(1). articles.title: No GST Under RCM on Honorarium Paid to Temple Board Members as They Are Not Directors: Kerala AAR
articles.summary: The Kerala Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that honorarium and sitting fees paid to members of statutory temple boards are not subject to Goods and Services Tax (GST) under the reverse charge mechanism (RCM). The decision clarifies that temple board members are not considered 'directors' as per GST notifications, exempting such payments from tax liability. This ruling impacts tax compliance for religious institutions and government bodies managing temple affairs.
articles.raw_summary: The Authority ruled that the President and Members of the statutory temple board are not “directors” under GST notifications. Accordingly, reverse charge GST was held inapplicable on honorarium and sitting fees. The post No GST Under RCM on Honorarium Paid to Temple Board Members as They Are Not Directors: Kerala AAR appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CESTAT Rejects Cenvat Credit Claim as Amount Was Not Paid Towards CVD
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) rejected a Cenvat credit claim because the amount was not paid towards Countervailing Duty (CVD). articles.title: GST ITC Cannot Be Denied Solely Due to NGTP Supplier Tag
articles.summary: GST authorities are increasingly denying Input Tax Credit (ITC) to buyers based on allegations of fraud in upstream transactions (NGTP suppliers) without proving the buyer's involvement in fraud. Courts have ruled that genuine buyers should not be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions, emphasizing that ITC cannot be denied solely due to such allegations. This impacts businesses relying on GST compliance and tax authorities' enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The article discusses how GST authorities are increasingly reversing ITC based on upstream NGTP allegations without proving fraud by the immediate buyer. It highlights judicial views that genuine buyers cannot be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions. articles.title: SC reiterates IBC can’t be used as tool for coercion, debt recovery in breach of contract cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a tool for coercion or debt recovery in cases that are primarily contractual disputes. The court upheld a ruling by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT), emphasizing that insolvency proceedings should not be invoked for breaches of contract. This decision reinforces the original intent of the IBC to address genuine insolvency situations rather than commercial disagreements.
articles.raw_summary: SC said insolvency proceedings can't be invoked for debt recovery in disputes that are predominantly contractual in nature. In doing so, it upheld an NCLAT ruling.

articles.title: NSE's Electronic Gold Receipts: What are EGRs and what is in them for gold buyers?
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has introduced Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs), allowing investors to hold gold in electronic form instead of physical form. This enables buyers to purchase small quantities (as little as 100 milligrams) and benefit from gold price movements. The system ensures gold purity and allows conversion back to physical gold or sale. The initiative is part of India's broader push toward digital financial instruments under SEBI's regulatory framework.
articles.raw_summary: EGRs give people the option to convert their physical gold into electronic format, hold it and enjoy the upside in gold prices, and later sell it or convert back to physical gold. The gold’s purity is assured and can be bought in small quantities of 100 milligrams.

The decision is part of efforts to strengthen the GST framework and reduce inconsistencies in tax-related legal interpretations.
articles.raw_summary: The move seeks to prevent smaller benches from deciding cases involving important legal questions, ensure consistent rulings, and streamline the process of resolving disputes.

articles.title: How outbreaks at sea have been helping to shape the global health system since medieval times

The article highlights how such outbreaks have influenced the development of public health frameworks worldwide, including those relevant to India's maritime and trade policies.

articles.title: DGFT tightens regulations for import of gold inputs by jewellers for exports
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has introduced stricter regulations for importing gold inputs used by jewellers for export purposes. articles.title: Less than 1% public vehicles have trackers, panic buttons. SC says no fitness certificate without them
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that public vehicles must have location tracking devices and emergency panic buttons installed to receive a fitness certificate. This requirement was mandated under the Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, following the Nirbhaya case. Currently, less than 1% of public vehicles comply with this rule. The court emphasized that no fitness certificate will be issued without these safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: Location tracking devices and emergency buttons had been made mandatory under Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, two years after Nirbhaya case.

The incident highlights concerns over the use of stringent laws in labour disputes and raises questions about police action and protest rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The family of one of the persons stated that she had been picked up by the police two days before the agitation for increased wages turned violent.

articles.raw_summary: The May 16 meeting of Tata Trusts has been deferred after the Charity Commissioner invoked provisions of the Maharashtra Public Trusts Act over alleged violations in trustee composition at the Sir Ratan Tata Trust. The meeting, which was expected to discuss governance matters at Tata Sons including board appointments and a possible listing, has now been postponed until further notice pending an ongoing inquiry into the complaints.

The directive follows the state's stance that bike taxis operate outside legal frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: The Maharashtra cyber cell told CNBC-TV18 that it is the responsibility of the app aggregators and the platforms to remove the 'bike taxi' option. "How they do that without disabling the entire app is not our problem. Bike tax is illegal and that needs to be removed," the Maharashtra cyber cell said.

articles.title: Can TVK’s Farm Promises Change Tamil Nadu Agriculture?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's farm politics is heating up ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections. The ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) government has focused on irrigation, subsidies, and welfare through a separate agriculture budget. In contrast, the opposition party TVK (led by Vijay) is promising direct economic relief to farmers, including crop loan waivers, higher minimum support prices (MSPs), and support for tenant farmers. These promises could shape future agricultural policies if implemented.
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu’s farm politics is entering a new phase ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections. While the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam government focused on irrigation, subsidies, mechanization and welfare through its separate agriculture budget, Vijay’s TVK is promising direct economic relief through crop loan waivers, higher MSPs and support for tenant farmers.

articles.title: Lowdown: What is the landmark US cryptocurrency regulation passed by the Senate Banking Committee
articles.summary: The US Senate Banking Committee advanced the CLARITY Act of 2025 in a bipartisan 15-9 vote, establishing regulatory rules for digital assets. The bill splits oversight between the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This marks a significant step in US cryptocurrency regulation, which could set a precedent for other countries, including India, in shaping their own digital asset policies.

articles.title: Sugar export ban to free up 4-5 lakh tonne for domestic use
articles.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 30. This move aims to increase domestic availability by 4-5 lakh tonnes. The decision is a response to El Nino concerns and potential impacts on future production. While boosting buffer stocks, the ban may affect sugar mill owners' short-term finances. Overseas demand had recently surged, but this ban redirects those supplies inward.

articles.title: Shapewear Finally Stopped Selling Transformation and Started Selling Infrastructure

What looks like a category evolution is, on closer inspection, a category reset.

articles.title: Shiv Sena row: SC irked over irresponsible statements by politicians, warns them to be careful
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed strong displeasure over politicians making irresponsible statements alleging that the court was not hearing the Shiv Sena symbol row case. A bench led by Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi cautioned that such conduct is unacceptable. The court noted that parties had sought dates in the matter, contradicting the allegations. The warning underscores the judiciary's authority and the need for responsible discourse on legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Friday expressed strong displeasure over "irresponsible statements" of some politicians alleging nonhearing of the Shiv Sena symbol row case in the top court, and cautioned that they should be careful as such conduct can't be accepted. A bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi observed that parties before it had sought dates in the matter and then statements were being made that the apex court was not deciding the case.

The issue may prompt discussions on human rights and gender equality in international forums, including potential relevance for Indian polic

The decree also addresses child marriage, divorce, and other marital disputes, granting fathers and grandfathers significant authority.

articles.title: India bans sugar exports till September to stabilise domestic prices
articles.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 30. This move aims to stabilize domestic prices as supply tightens. The ban applies to raw, white, and refined sugar. Production forecasts are also impacted by weather patterns.

articles.title: HeLa to a new world where progress in science matches dignity for patient
articles.summary: Henrietta Lacks' family successfully fought for recognition of her contribution to biomedical research after her cells were used without consent. Their efforts led to stricter regulations governing ethical research, ensuring patient dignity and informed consent in scientific studies. The case highlights the importance of ethical standards in medical research and the need for regulatory oversight to protect patient rights.
articles.raw_summary: The omission of Henrietta Lacks, who donated the immortal cells that have facilitated biomedical research, from scientific history is a rare wrong that has been righted, recently. Thanks to the battle her family fought, today, more regulations will govern ethical research.

articles.title: Unemployment rate hits six-month high of 5.2% in April
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to 5.2% in April, a six-month peak. Rural joblessness saw an increase, while urban unemployment eased to a year-low. Urban female unemployment declined significantly. Youth unemployment edged up, with a sharp rise for young women. Labour force participation and the worker population ratio also saw a slight decrease.
articles.raw_summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to 5.2% in April, a six-month peak. Rural joblessness saw an increase, while urban unemployment eased to a year-low. Urban female unemployment declined significantly. Youth unemployment edged up, with a sharp rise for young women. Labour force participation and the worker population ratio also saw a slight decrease.

articles.title: India’s unemployment rate steady at 5.2% in April; rural joblessness rises to 4.6%
articles.summary: India's job market shows stability. The overall unemployment rate was 5.2% in April 2026. Rural joblessness increased slightly. Urban unemployment saw a small decrease. Labour force participation declined for both men and women. Worker population ratio also moderated. These trends indicate minor shifts in employment conditions across the country.

articles.title: India spots an export champion in a sector tariffs failed to sink
articles.summary: India's seafood sector has demonstrated remarkable resilience, transforming a 50% US tariff into a catalyst for growth. Through strategic market diversification, regulatory diplomacy, and domestic innovation, the industry achieved record exports in FY26. The government is now considering a production-linked incentive scheme to further boost value-added exports and expand the exporter base, solidifying India's position as a global powerhouse.

articles.title: Wholesale inflation hits 42-month high of 8.3% in April
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation reached an 8.3% peak in April, a 42-month high. Rising fuel, power, and crude oil prices fueled this surge. Experts predict inflation may climb further in May. Disruptions in West Asia and potential El Nino effects are key concerns. Consumers are shielded from retail fuel price hikes, but commercial LPG costs increased.

articles.title: India wholesale inflation jumps to 8.3%, highest in 3-1/2 years on oil shock
articles.summary: Wholesale inflation in India jumped to 8.3 percent in April. This is the highest rate recorded in the current series. Soaring fuel and crude oil prices are the main drivers. This rise impacts mineral oils, natural gas, and manufactured goods. Producer costs are increasing rapidly. Global commodity and energy prices are affecting domestic supply chains.

articles.title: FPIs pull out Rs 27,000 cr in May; 2026 outflows hit Rs 2.2 lakh cr-mark
articles.summary: Himanshu Srivastava, Principal - Manager Research at Morningstar Investment Research India, said the latest outflow trend reflected persistent uncertainty surrounding global growth, elevated geopolitical tensions across key regions and volatility in crude oil prices, which continued to weigh on risk appetite towards emerging markets, including India.

articles.title: India scrambles to steady rupee as oil shock bites
articles.summary: India is scrambling to salvage a sinking rupee as surging oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict threaten to disrupt the world's fastest-growing major economy. The depreciation has punctured India's ambition to become the world's third-largest economy.

articles.title: India missed out on AI, and now its run as market darling may be over
articles.summary: India's stock market faces a significant downturn as global investment shifts towards AI. Foreign investors are leaving, impacting market value. The country's IT sector, a major driver of past success, is now vulnerable to AI automation. This presents a critical juncture for India's economic future and its position in global markets.

articles.title: India’s gold import duty hike: A double-edged sword
articles.summary: India has raised gold import duty to 15% and urged citizens to buy less gold. This move targets foreign reserves and the rupee's value. However, India's strong cultural demand for gold may limit effectiveness. Higher duties could also boost illegal gold imports. Investors should consider gold as a long-term hedge.

articles.title: Fuel hike not enough to offset all losses, oil stocks fall further
articles.summary: Shares of oil companies in India fell despite a recent fuel price hike. The adjustment did not fully offset losses caused by rising global crude oil prices. Experts suggest further pricing changes may be needed, while investors are advised to wait for clearer market signals. Persistent geopolitical tensions continue to support high oil prices, posing challenges for company profitability.

This adjustment couldn't compensate for the rising crude oil prices, leaving experts calling for further alterations in pricing. Investors are advised to hold tight until market indicators become more distinct. The persistent geopolitical climate is keeping oil prices robust, creating challenges for company profitability.

articles.title: AI becoming infrastructure of intelligence: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is now the core operating system for businesses worldwide. TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran stated AI services are generating billions in revenue. TCS also reported strong profit growth and job additions.

articles.title: TCS CEO K Krithivasan takes home over Rs 28 crore in FY26 annual pay package
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services CEO   MD K Krithivasan's remuneration reached over Rs 28 crore in FY26, a 6.3% increase year-on-year. This figure includes salary, benefits, and a significant commission. The company also disclosed remuneration for COO Aarthi Subramanian and CFO Samir Seksaria, alongside Rs 1,388 crore in restructuring expenses for organizational transformation.

articles.title: At Samsung, the global AI boom spurred a looming strike and deep divisions
articles.summary: Over 45,000 workers at Samsung Electronics in South Korea are threatening a major strike, demanding a fair share of profits from the AI boom. The dispute could disrupt global production of memory chips used in AI data centers, smartphones, and laptops. The Indian government and investors are concerned about potential economic fallout and supply chain instability. The unrest also exposes deep divisions within the company over worker compensation.

The dispute could disrupt memory chip production, impacting AI data centers, smartphones, and laptops globally. Government and investors express concern over potential economic fallout and supply chain instability. This labor unrest highlights deep divisions within the tech giant.

articles.title: Indian startups must avoid ‘recency bias’ while building for an AI-first world: OpenAI’s Pragya Misra
articles.summary: OpenAI's Pragya Misra urges Indian startups to look beyond immediate needs, focusing on future AI-driven problems. She highlighted India as a crucial testing ground for new AI features, emphasising that building for its diverse user base prepares companies for global scale. Technical depth remains vital in the AI era.
articles.raw_summary: Indian startups must look beyond immediate needs and build for future AI challenges. India's ability to create products for diverse users offers a global scaling advantage.

articles.title: D2C brands feel the pinch as consumers cut spending
articles.summary: Rising input costs and shrinking demand are squeezing smaller brands, with consumer spending projected to drop 5-6% next quarter. D2C brands face pressure as inflation and a weakening rupee push consumers towards essential purchases, impacting discretionary spending and average order values. Companies are absorbing costs or planning price hikes up to 10%.

The company also said it has no immediate plans for fresh fundraising.

articles.title: Brookfield sees long-term opportunity in India despite market volatility
articles.summary: Brookfield had set a target of reaching $100 billion in assets under management by 2030, and Deputy Global Chief Investment Officer Ankur Gupta says the firm is on track — and may get there earlier than planned. He said the goal remains unchanged and credited the strong compounding effect of Brookfield's existing business portfolio in India as a key reason for that confidence.

articles.title: OPEC lowers 2026 oil demand outlook, raises 2027 growth forecast
articles.summary: OPEC has reduced its 2026 oil demand growth forecast to 1.17 million barrels per day (bpd) and lowered its Q2 2026 demand estimate, citing volatile economic conditions. However, it raised the 2027 growth outlook to 1.54 million bpd. The changes reflect shifting global energy trends that may impact India's energy security and import strategies.

articles.title: Fuel price hike inevitable as oil crisis deepens; experts warn worst is yet to come
articles.summary: Industry experts have warned that India’s oil crisis could worsen in the coming months as elevated crude prices, a weak rupee and mounting losses at oil marketing companies increase the likelihood of a retail fuel price hike. Analysts also cautioned that prolonged high energy costs may push inflation higher and weigh on economic growth.

articles.title: India April CPI inflation hits 13-month high at 3.48%, stays below 4% mark
articles.summary: India’s retail inflation rose to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, driven by higher food and services prices, though the headline print remained below the 4% mark. Food inflation accelerated to 4.20% from 3.87% in March, while core inflation edged up to 3.4%.
articles.raw_summary: India’s retail inflation rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March, driven by higher food and services prices, though the reading remained below CNBC-TV18’s poll estimate of 3.72%. The latest print marks a 13-month high under the revised CPI series, while under the older series it would have been a four-month high.

articles.title: China’s factory inflation surges to a near 4-year high; domestic demand remains tepid
articles.summary: China's factory prices rose by 2.8% in April, the fastest increase since July 2022, ending a period of deflation. The surge is linked to disruptions in energy supplies due to the Iran war, but weak domestic demand and a sluggish labor market are limiting firms' ability to raise prices. The situation highlights broader economic challenges in China that could affect global trade and energy markets.
